jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 3+ years of data engineering experience
- 4+ years of SQL experience
- Experience with data modeling, warehousing and building ETL pipelines
- Experience with at least one programming language (Python, Java, Scala).
- Proven ability to work with large-scale datasets and optimize performance
jobs.description:
Join the Amazon Payments Products - North America Credit (APD-NAC) Data Engineering team, where we build the data infrastructure powering Amazon's credit card businesses. Our team delivers data-driven solutions that impact millions of customers across Amazon's payment ecosystem. We're a high-performing team that consistently delivers while maintaining zero-defect standards through rigorous engineering practices. We're at the forefront of AI-first transformation, implementing cutting-edge automation solutions and next-generation data architectures that drive measurable productivity gains. Our work spans above and beyond the critical business areas such as customer acquisition, fraud detection, product recommendations, financial and executive reporting.

As a Data Engineer II, you'll work on high-impact projects (targeting millions of customers), large-scale customer acquisition initiatives, and fraud detection platforms. You'll collaborate with Product Managers, Data Scientists, Business Intelligence Engineers, UX Designers, Software Development Engineers, GMs, and other cross-functional engineering teams to solve complex data challenges at massive scale while driving innovation through AI-powered automation.


Key job responsibilities
Design, build, and optimize logical data models and data pipelines for complex datasets
Own ongoing data quality and create self-service access to datasets for business intelligence
Work on major portions of existing or new data architecture within the team
Collaborate with Software Development Engineers and other Data Engineers to design stable, performant data solutions
Write secure, stable, testable, and maintainable code with minimal defects
Apply appropriate data design approaches and make judicious trade-offs without over-engineering
Optimize resource usage including system hardware, data storage, query optimization, and AWS infrastructure
Participate actively in code reviews, design discussions, and team planning
Mentor and train junior team members and new peers
Resolve root causes of complex problems and balance customer requirements with team needs
Stay current on distributed systems technologies (MapReduce, MPP architectures, NoSQL databases)

A day in the life
As a DE II, you'll leverage your expertise in distributed systems and data technologies to solve difficult problems and contribute meaningfully to the team's data architecture while maintaining high standards of operational excellence.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 2+ years of technical work experience, or Bachelor's degree in engineering, statistics, computer science, operations research, business analytics, information systems or equivalent
- Knowledge of writing and optimizing SQL queries in a business environment with large-scale, complex datasets
- Bachelor's degree in engineering, statistics or business, or a CPA and experience in Excel (macros, index, conditional list, arrays, pivots, lookups)
- Experience with reporting and Data Visualization tools such as Quick Sight / Tableau / Power BI or other BI packages
- Salesforce familiarity: working knowledge of Salesforce objects, reports, dashboards.
jobs.description:
About the Team and the Role
Join the Amazon IN Ads team as a key operational partner to the Sales team. You will deliver accurate, scalable reporting, dash-boarding, and automation solutions while providing day-to-day support — triaging requests, resolving issues with minimal back-and-forth, and responding to incidents within defined SLAs. The role demands a first time-right mindset: translating sales team needs into reliable data outputs, automated workflows, and stable operations with minimum iterations. Core skills include hands-on SQL querying, Excel VBA development, dash-boarding, and Salesforce operational reporting, with a sharp focus on eliminating manual toil, ensuring data accuracy at scale, and maintaining operational continuity.

Key job responsibilities
Day-to-Day Responsibilities
Provide day-to-day operational support, triage incoming requests, and respond to incidents within defined SLAs.
• Act as the first point of contact for data issues — ensuring timely resolution and stakeholder communication.
• Maintain and track incident logs, escalate where needed, and drive follow-through to closure.
• Monitor operational health of reports, dashboards, and automated workflows to proactively identify and resolve issues before they impact stakeholders.

A day in the life
• Automate repetitive workflows and reporting pipelines using Excel VBA macros, SQL stored procedures, and Salesforce Flows.
• Troubleshoot production data/reporting/dashboard issues, perform root cause analysis, and implement sustainable fixes to reduce recurring incidents and SLA breaches.
• Support operational processes including data uploads, bulk updates, and scheduled report distribution while monitoring system health.
Process Improvement
• Identify automation opportunities by analyzing existing manual workflows, recurring incidents, and SLA patterns to propose automated workflows.
• Document all dashboards, reports, queries, macros, and workflows to ensure knowledge continuity and team scalability.


About the team
The Amazon India Ads business functions as a strategic start-up with support and interest from business units across the company as well as Amazon’s top management. We are investing heavily in building a world class advertising business and are responsible for defining and delivering a collection of advertising products that drive awareness, engagement, discovery and sales. We deliver billions of ad impressions and millions of clicks daily and are breaking fresh ground to create new, cutting edge ad products. We are highly motivated, collaborative and fun-loving with an entrepreneurial spirit and bias for action.

jobs.description:
The AWS Science of Security team is dedicated to making AWS the best computing service in the world for customers who require advanced and rigorous solutions for security, privacy, and sovereignty.

The ideal candidate thrives on tackling ambiguity and complexity. You are a big-picture thinker who can operate in a startup environment. You will work with partner teams, engineers, and scientists to develop deep understanding of not only the product strategy but also the underlying technology. You will create the roadmap to incrementally solve complex problems that allow AWS to set the industry bar on security and compliance.

This is a unique and rare opportunity to get in early on a fast-growing segment of AWS and help shape the product vision and business. You will have a chance to utilize your deep technical and product experience in a fast moving start-up environment and have positive business and customer impact.

About the team
Diverse Experiences
Amazon Security values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why Amazon Security?
At Amazon, security is central to maintaining customer trust and delivering delightful customer experiences. Our organization is responsible for creating and maintaining a high bar for security across all of Amazon’s products and services. We offer talented security professionals the chance to accelerate their careers with opportunities to build experience in a wide variety of areas including cloud, devices, retail, entertainment, healthcare, operations, and physical stores.

Inclusive Team Culture
In Amazon Security, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Ongoing DEI events and learning experiences inspire us to continue learning and to embrace our uniqueness. Addressing the toughest security challenges requires that we seek out and celebrate a diversity of ideas, perspectives, and voices.

Training & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, training, and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why flexible work hours and arrangements are part of our culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

jobs.description:
Want to be part of a dynamic start-up environment and aspire to build high quality, resilient, low latency and innovative solutions which can scale for next 100M payers? If yes, we at Amazon Pay India are in process of building greenfield solution which can scale to next level. We aspire to design solutions which scale for every Indian and help grow our business multi-fold in near future to revolutionise Digital Payments in India by making every payment transaction trusted, convenient and rewarding. Amazon Pay India org is in process of expanding their footprint in NCR regions and looking for passionate, experienced, talented engineers with the desire to make big difference in a fast-paced environment. This is a rare opportunity to be part of the team that will be responsible for building a successful, sustainable, strategic and independently profitable business at 10x scale in next 2-3 years. The candidate will be working to shape the product, innovate on behalf of customers and will be actively involved in defining key product features that impact our business. You will work with highly talented engineers to evolve the design and implementation of the products owned by this team and responsible for setting up and holding a high-quality bar in a highly technical team of Software Engineers. Team Impact Amazon Pay Rewards Team has a bold vision to make every customer transaction rewarding and every reward financially sustainable. To execute on this vision, Amazon India Rewards team is systematically investing and building product innovation in areas of instant bank discount, No Cost EMI and Exchange Discount . During large promotional events like Diwali, these are the key customer levers to drive shoppers to Amazon. As a result, Amazon Pay has grown rapidly. It has enabled over 5 million neighbourhood stores. It’s revenue grew over 64 per cent to Rs 1,370 crore over Rs 834.5 crore in fiscal 2019. You work will have a deep impact on how millions of customers shop on Amazon. Amazon Pay Rewards was launched in Mar 2019. In less than three years, we had major positive impact on customer acquisition, engagement and retention strategy for Amazon India. Customers love the rewards that we offer and our product is used by tens of millions of users in India. This roles allows you to work on product and services that are closely connected to customers and you will have the opportunity to be both the customers and the builder. Your role As a developer in this team you will work with highly talented team of engineers, managers, product owners and designers to build and improve customer facing features like Instant Bank Discount, No Cost EMI and Exchange Discount. If you are looking for a creative, startup-like work environment and want to be at the center of Amazon innovation, this is the place to be! Our engineers own the rewards experience and work on a wide range of technologies (including Lambda, ECS, EDP, SQS, Dynamo DB, Slapshot, Herd, Horizonte etc.) and build and operate highly scalable, low latency mobile first products and services. You will take ownership over the software design, documentation, development, engineering approach, delivery and support of the rewards platform. In this role you will collaborate with leaders, work backwards from customers, influence product requirements, identify problems, propose innovative solutions, relentlessly raise standards. Apart of technical challenges, you will also get a chance to work on mathematical, economic and gamification models to engage more customers in an economically sustainable way.


Key job responsibilities
1. Own writing and maintaining comprehensive test strategy/plans, test cases and execute the same to assure quality of ShipTech software releases
2. Work closely with SDEs, Product Managers, TPMs, QAEs, SDETs, and other stakeholders to ensure a seamless integration of quality throughout the development lifecycle.
3. Experience programming with at least one modern language such as Java, C++, or C# including object-oriented design to design and debug/maintain test automation scripts.
4. Create, maintain, and execute test cases, test scripts, and test scenarios for various software components, including functional, regression, performance, and security testing
5. Perform initial defect triage and root cause analysis, correctly log defects and collaborate with cross-functional teams so they can address issues.
6. Strong oral and written communication skills, as well as the ability to think clearly and analytically, problem solving, scope business requirements, and prioritize work on projects.
7. Actively participate in retrospectives and contribute to process improvements to enhance overall product quality.

jobs.company_name: Amazon Servicos de Varejo do Brasil Ltda. - C41
jobs.description:
Revolutionize Global Operations with Amazon OTS

At Amazon's core lies our Operations team, renowned for delivering excellence at unprecedented speed and scale. Every day, our facilities process and deliver millions of products to customers across the globe. OTS - OpsTech Solutions serves as the backbone of this massive operation, providing 24/7 IT support to keep our worldwide network running seamlessly.

Your Role as IT Support Engineer I

Join us in shaping the future of e-commerce technology. As an IT Support Engineer I, you'll:

Deploy innovative technical solutions across regional facilities
Provide expert support for hardware, operating systems, and enterprise applications
Think creatively to solve unique challenges beyond standard procedures
Collaborate with global teams to maintain and improve our technology infrastructure
Drive operational excellence through hands-on problem-solving

Why Amazon OTS?

We offer more than just a job:

Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits from day one
Healthcare coverage that starts immediately
Casual, practical work environment (yes, jeans and t-shirts are welcome!)
Opportunity to work with cutting-edge technology
Career growth in a dynamic, global environment

Join our team of problem-solvers who take pride in powering the world's most customer-centric company.

Help us write the next chapter in Amazon's technological evolution.

Key job responsibilities
Key Responsibilities:

Technical Operations
• Drive network excellence through advanced engineering, infrastructure management, and systems administration
• Maintain critical systems uptime and performance during operational hours
• Execute local technical projects from conception to completion

Problem-Solving & Process Improvement
• Conduct root cause analysis to resolve operational challenges
• Design and implement efficient IT solutions and processes
• Develop and maintain technical documentation and standard procedures

Customer Support & Collaboration
• Partner with leadership and teams to enhance IT infrastructure
• Provide expert technical support across multiple facilities
• Deliver remote assistance to distributed teams
• Travel within region to support on-site needs as required

This position combines hands-on technical expertise with strategic problem-solving to ensure seamless operations across our network of facilities. The ideal candidate thrives in a dynamic environment and is passionate about delivering innovative IT solutions.

jobs.description:
Amazon Photos is seeking a Principal Technical Program Manager to define and deliver important programs with broad cross-organizational impact across Amazon Photos services. Your work will focus on very large engineering efforts that solve significantly complex or endemic problems. You will be trusted to operate with complete independence and will often be assigned to focus on areas where the business and/or architectural strategy has not yet been defined.

Key job responsibilities
- Strategic Leadership: Define and deliver programs with broad cross-organizational, cross-business, or significant technology impact across multiple VP organizations
- Architectural Influence: Drive architecture and/or organization changes that enable teams to work independently and achieve significant efficiency improvements
- Complex Problem Solving: Manage significantly complex cross-functional technology initiatives, remediating critical and/or endemic problems
- Technical Excellence: Apply deep technical knowledge to improve technologies at massive scale (content processing, ML model serving, foundational services)
- Stakeholder Management: Partner effectively with all levels of leadership, including VPs; manage escalations and represent complex decisions clearly to leaders up to 3 levels above
- Force Multiplication: Effectively force multiply by creating and optimizing critical mechanisms that ensure long-term program success
- Risk Management: Assess risk, drive clarity, and foster shared understanding by asking intelligent questions about proposed solutions
- Cross-Organizational Coordination: Identify gaps/opportunities between architectures and organizations (services, workflows, tooling) across the Amazon domains
- Process Innovation: Align teams and orgs toward simple, coherent approaches; create/optimize cross-org structures and mechanisms
- Talent Development: Mentor team members, perform TPM promotion assessments, and participate in talent reviews

A day in the life
As a Principal TPM, you will own the technology roadmap for a broad set of systems. You will lead the design, implementation, and delivery of highly complicated cross-functional projects in ambiguous or new technology areas. Your role requires driving strategic alignment across engineering, product, business, and device teams—including integrations with Echo Show, Fire TV, Fire Tablet, and future Amazon devices.

You will proactively drive business and system-level reviews, aligning teams toward coherent technology strategies and effective engineering processes. You'll identify gaps and opportunities across architectures and organizations, decomposing complex problems into straightforward solutions while minimizing redundant engineering efforts year-over-year.

About the team
Amazon Photos helps consumers preserve and re-live their memories. The service comes with unlimited photo storage for Prime members and apps are available on iOS, Android, Web, Desktop, Fire Tablet & Fire TV. The service is also integrated into Alexa devices such as Echo Show. We have ambitious goals to scale growth and adoption of the service around the world and delight our customers with a great product.

jobs.description:
We are looking for a dynamic technology leader who will be responsible for leading our product and user experience teams. This leader will define and execute innovative initiatives that will improve the way Amazon customer interact and utilize Alexa while in in their car. The role involves thinking big, building foundational solutions while innovating on customer experience. It also involves helping with the career development of others, actively mentoring individuals and driving initiatives to guide the career growth of their team members.

As a Principal Product Manager you will be a leader with massive impact. You’ll help establish product and UX standards and drive Amazon Smart Vehicle's overall product roadmap and best in class product development practices. You’ll work on Amazon’s hardest problems, building high quality, customer focused products that are aligned with our business needs. You’ll think globally when building, ensuring Amazon builds high performing, scalable systems that fit well together. Amazon Principal Product Managers are visionaries who can translate business needs into workable technology solutions. Your expertise is deep and broad; you are hands on, while being able to drive a long term strategy.


Key job responsibilities
- Serve as a product lead on our most demanding, cross-functional projects.
- Ensure the quality of our design systems that meet best in class customer experience.
- Decompose complex problems into simple, straight-forward solutions.
- Fully and completely understand system inter-dependencies and limitations.
- Possess expert knowledge in product scalability and product management best practices.
- Effectively research and benchmark Amazon technology against other competing systems in the industry.
- Drive product strategy in a clear and concise manner across partner internal and external teams.
- Be the ultimate champion for the end customer to ensure that our external partners meet the high bar of being an Amazon Alexa product.
- Assist in the career development of others, actively mentoring individuals and the community on product issues and helping your team on career growth.
- Exert influence over multiple teams, increasing their productivity and effectiveness by sharing your deep knowledge and experience.

About the team
Here at Amazon, we believe voice is a big part of the future. It’s only been a few years since we launched Echo and Alexa, but customers are already using Alexa to do all kinds of things. As Alexa has become more natural and more useful for customers, we’ve started looking at ways to extend that experience beyond the home. Our goal is to make Alexa available to customers wherever they want to interact with her, and auto represents an especially exciting opportunity for us. Amazon Smart Vehicles' team partners with automakers, suppliers, and solution integrators. We are at the forefront of an automotive disruption driven by connectivity, autonomy, shared ownership, and electrification and our engagements span 100-year-old companies (BMW, Ford, Toyota) and startups (Rivian, Lucid, Fisker).

jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

Amazon Web Services (AWS) provides a highly reliable, scalable, and low-cost cloud platform that powers thousands of businesses in over 190 countries. AWS’ Infrastructure Supply Chain & Procurement (ISCaP) organization works to deliver solutions to source, build and maintain our socially responsible data center supply chains. We are a team of highly-motivated, engaged, and responsive professionals who enable the core sustainable infrastructure of AWS.

We are seeking a highly effective supply chain professional to manage one of our most complex commodities. In this role, the candidate will manage a semiconductor supply chain with multiple products in close collaboration with both internal and external cross functional teams to help enable AWS’s ISCaP team achieve continuity of supply and cost goals.

The successful candidate must be a self-starter comfortable with ambiguity, with high attention to detail, and a proven ability to work in a fast-paced and ever-changing environment. Candidates must have excellent analytical capabilities, program management experience, be comfortable diving deep into data, have strong writing skills, lead executive-level reviews, and communicate clearly and effectively to all levels of the company, both in writing and in meetings.

The ideal candidate will not only raise the bar on delivering the right supply at the right time by deep diving into data, but is a person who actively participates with a strong curiosity to understand the business end-to-end, and has a track record of using data to influence decision makers.

In this role, you will:
- Serve as a key member of the AWS Infrastructure supply chain team in helping to define and deliver complex supply chain strategies.
- Design, develop and maintain scalable and reliable analytical tools, dashboards, and metrics that drive key decisions and resource prioritization.
- Understand the technical requirements of our partners and work closely with the internal technical teams to guide the direction of activities that will impact our supply chain.
- Produce written recommendations and insights for key stakeholders to help shape solution design.
- Handle multiple projects at once, deal with ambiguity and rapidly-changing priorities.
- Design, develop and maintain scalable and reliable analytical tools, dashboards, and metrics that drive key decisions and resource prioritization.
Our environment is fast-paced and requires someone who is hard-working, meticulous with high bias for action, who gets things done at an effective pace, gets results, and is comfortable working with tight deadlines and changing priorities.




Key job responsibilities
Global Supply Managers (GSMs) are responsible for maintaining continuity of supply for the categories and / or components they manage. They will develop clear action plans to drive global supply availability and execute on supply chain strategies. They will lead internal engagements across Supply Chain, Engineering, NPI, Logistics, Planning and Business organizations while engaging closely with suppliers on supply monitoring and status. They will manage supplier relationships and communication, track orders and inventory levels, process purchase orders, ensure timely delivery, coordinate material requirements with internal teams, maintain accurate procurement documentation, and lead cross-functional initiatives while ensuring timely delivery and quality standards across global operations. The ideal candidate will demonstrate strong ownership, cross-functional collaboration, and a proven ability to deliver measurable results in a fast-paced environment.

A day in the life
Key Responsibilities / Daily Activities include:
• Material/inventory Planning
• Supply/Demand planning / forecasting
• Maintaining continuity of supply (COS)
• Communicating supply status, risks/mitigations to internal stakeholders and executive leadership
• Working with data and analytics (ability to view, edit, and extrapolate data in Excel or similar spreadsheet software)
• Working with suppliers/vendors, inventory warehousing teams
• Managing electromechanical component supply chain (Power Supply Units, Inverters, batteries, racks, frames, mechanical accessories, chassis, fans, heatsinks etc.)
• Capacity planning (expanding new manufacturing lines)

On a typical day, you will:

- Perform forecasting, demand analysis, material/inventory management, and manage supply buffers
- Understand manufacturing processes to verify supply commitments and reduce cycle times
- Work with component suppliers and server manufacturers to analyze, reconcile allocation and monitor changes and cancellations
- Participate in requirements gathering
- Understand the supply chain network and work with various analytics and software development teams, driving automation
- Develop analytical tools and dashboards to track metrics, monitor SLAs, and report findings
- Produce written recommendations and insights to inform key stakeholders of supply chain health and help shape solution design

You will be responsible for:
- Forecast generation and demand planning
- Allocations of AWS material from inventory hubs to suppliers
- Requirement and Specification reviews with Hardware Engineering
- Ocean, air, and ground transportation planning
- Supply heatmap generation

About the team
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Diverse Experiences
Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship and Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.
